measure_name: Implementation of minimum energy performance standards (MEPS) in the tertiary sector
objective: According to Article 9 of the EPBD, the main objective of this measure is to ensure that all non-residential buildings are below a certain maximum energy performance threshold. In addition, and in accordance with Article 5 of the EPBD, this measure also aims to ensure that minimum energy performance requirements are set for buildings or building units in order to achieve at least cost-optimal levels and, where appropriate, more stringent benchmarks, such as the requirements for nearly zero-energy buildings and zero-emission buildings.
planned_budget_and_sources: EUR 800.000,00 from the budget of the AGE.
entities_responsible: MIVAU, MITERD, IDAE, IETcc

measure_name: Regulatory framework for the promotion of energy renovation
objective: In accordance with Article 8 of the EPBD, the objective of this measure is to ensure that when buildings undergo major renovation, the energy performance of the building or the renovated part thereof is upgraded in order to meet minimum energy performance requirements, provided that it is technically, functionally and economically feasible.
planned_budget_and_sources: The actions described in this measure are carried out directly with the own resources of the competent administrations involved and do not entail additional expenditure for them.
entities_responsible: MIVAU, MITERD, IDAE, IETcc, CCAA and EELL

measure_name: Public aid for energy renovation
objective: In accordance with Art. 17 of the EPBD, the objective of this measure is to ensure that appropriate funding, support measures and other instruments are available from the public domain to address market barriers in order to deliver the necessary investments to transform its building stock into zero-emission buildings by 2050 at the latest.
planned_budget_and_sources: EUR 0.000,00 from the budget of the General State Administration and EUR 487.200.000,00 from the budgets of the Autonomous Communities.
entities_responsible: MIVAU, MITERD, IDAE, CCAA, LRA

measure_name: Tax incentives to promote energy renovation
objective: In accordance with Art. 17 of the EPBD, the objective of this measure is to ensure that support measures and other appropriate instruments are in place from the public domain to address market barriers in order to deliver the necessary investments to transform its building stock into zero-emission buildings by 2050 at the latest.
planned_budget_and_sources: EUR 225.600.000,00 from the AGE budget.
entities_responsible: Ministry of Finance, Autonomous Communities and local authorities

measure_name: Promotion of renovation of rented housing
objective: In accordance with Article 17 of the EPBD, the objective of this measure is to ensure an appropriate distribution of the benefits and costs of all energy renovation actions presenting split incentives, such as that where financial incentives are provided to owners of buildings or building units for the renovation of their rented buildings or building units, those incentives benefit both owners and tenants.
planned_budget_and_sources: EUR 324.000.000,00 from the AGE budget.
entities_responsible: AGE, Autonomous Communities and local authorities

measure_name: Monitoring and follow-up of compliance with energy efficiency obligations
objective: In accordance with the EPBD, which places particular emphasis on the monitoring to be carried out of the measures available, the objective of this measure is to ensure proper monitoring and follow-up of the main energy efficiency and sustainability indicators presented by the building stock, in order to ensure proper design and prioritisation of actions, correct assessment of the effectiveness of the measures implemented and increased reliability as regards compliance with the objectives established by the Directive.
planned_budget_and_sources: EUR 400.000,00 from the budget of the AGE.
entities_responsible: MIVAU, MITERD, IDAE, IETcc

measure_name: Promoting deep renovation through energy regulations and technical building regulations
objective: In accordance with Art. 12 of the EPBD, the objective of this measure is to encourage deep renovation of buildings, and the appropriate establishment of a system for staged deep renovation.

entities_responsible: MIVAU, MITERD, IDAE, IETcc, CCAA, EELL

measure_name: Financial support and fiscal incentives to promote deep renovation in one or more steps

entities_responsible: AGE policy, the Autonomous Communities and local authorities

measure_name: Amendment of the existing regulatory framework incorporating the new aspects of the carbon footprint applied to buildings
objective: The GWP over the whole life cycle of a building indicates the building's overall contribution to the emissions that cause climate change. The EPBD provides for this indicator to account for all greenhouse gas emissions that occur when a building is constructed, from emissions embodied in construction products to emissions and externalities that occur at the end of a building's useful life, as well as direct and indirect emissions generated during the use stage.
planned_budget_and_sources: The actions described in this measure are carried out directly with the own resources of the competent administrations involved and do not entail additional expenditure for them.
entities_responsible: MIVAU, MITERD, IETcc policy

measure_name: Setting global warming potential limits for new buildings and extensions of existing ones
objective: The EPBD requires Member States to set limit values on the total cumulative GWP over the life cycle of buildings, with targets to be set from 2030 onwards, in line with the Union's objective to reach climate neutrality by 2050. To this end, national roadmaps detailing how to introduce such thresholds should be drawn up and notified to the European Commission.
